description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone block with a roughly rectangular outline and a weathered, natural surface. The front face features a shallow relief carving depicting a human figure, possibly from an ancient or classical context, with discernible facial features and an arm extended. The sides are rough and uneven, showing no signs of modern cutting or finishing, while the back is unadorned and appears more eroded. The stone has a pale, off-white coloration typical of marble or limestone, with subtle variations in tone and texture. The block is relatively thin compared to its height and width, suggesting it may have once been part of a larger architectural or sculptural element. Its overall appearance suggests historical significance and durability, suitable for use as a decorative or commemorative urban feature.
description: An irregular, pale stone block with a carved human relief on one face and rough, weathered surfaces on the other sides.
description_view_0: The front view shows the shallow relief carving of a human figure, with the most detail and visual interest.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thinness of the block and its rough, uncarved edge.
description_view_2: The back view displays an unadorned, natural stone surface with no carvings or markings.
description_view_3: The right side view again highlights the block's thin profile and irregular, rough edge.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or plaza with commemorative sculptures
attribute_installation_method: set upright on a prepared stone or concrete base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with softened edges and some erosion, especially on the uncarved sides.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible shallow relief carving of a human figure on the front face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely displayed as a standalone commemorative or decorative element
